changes.legacy_state_or_collapse: The shared YAML loader reports syntax, constructor ValueError, constructor OverflowError, data-model, and resource-limit failures through prose-oriented exception families reused at adoption-document and register callsites.
changes.proposed_v0_5_distinction: F0005 identifies only YAML grammar or constructor syntax/value failures for an adoption-policy or register document; invalid UTF-8, invalid data model, and bounded-resource exhaustion remain distinct identities.
changes.rationale: A grammar failure is a completed bounded-parse result and must not acquire the identity of a schema violation or a resource-limit failure merely because v0.4 rendered them through the same loader path.
changes.compatibility_impact: A legacy generic YAML-invalid result can no longer be mapped by message text alone; the producer must expose its exception variant and callsite before emitting F0005.
changes.migration_note: At load_yaml callsites 3275 and 3620, map YAML_SYNTAX_OR_CONSTRUCTOR_INVALID, YAML_CONSTRUCTOR_VALUE_INVALID, and YAML_CONSTRUCTOR_OVERFLOW_INVALID to F0005; route UTF-8, data-model, and limit variants to their own codes.

authority_locators.locator: semantic_mapping groups U-1255 and U-1257 at load_yaml@3275 and load_yaml@3620

changes.legacy_state_or_collapse: YamlDataModelError is caught inside the same v0.4 loader branch that also catches constructor values, complexity failures, recursion, and overflow, so its stable semantic identity is absent from the rendered result.
changes.proposed_v0_5_distinction: F0007 identifies a successfully parsed YAML value that falls outside the permitted JSON-compatible policy data model, at either the adoption-document or register-document callsite.
changes.rationale: An invalid value model is neither malformed YAML nor exhaustion of a bounded parser budget and therefore needs an independent remediation and identity.
changes.compatibility_impact: Consumers that grouped every v0.4 loader ValueError together must recognize F0007 separately from F0005 and F0008.
changes.migration_note: Emit F0007 only for the YAML_DATA_MODEL_INVALID producer variant at load_yaml@3275 or load_yaml@3620; do not infer it from rendered exception prose.

authority_locators.locator: semantic_mapping group U-1257 variant YAML_DATA_MODEL_INVALID at load_yaml@3275 and load_yaml@3620

changes.legacy_state_or_collapse: v0.4 exposes byte size, numeric-token length, alias expansion, expanded node count, and recursion exhaustion through several loader branches without one stable resource-limit identity or a reliable limit-kind fact.
changes.proposed_v0_5_distinction: F0008 represents bounded YAML resource exhaustion and carries a closed limit_kind fact for BYTE_SIZE, NUMERIC_VALUE, NESTING, ALIAS_EXPANSION, or NODE_COUNT.
changes.rationale: Resource exhaustion has operational and security consequences distinct from invalid syntax, and the exceeded budget must be machine-visible without parsing the error string.
changes.compatibility_impact: Several v0.4 messages collapse into one code plus a required fact; adapters that cannot produce a valid limit_kind must fail closed instead of guessing.
changes.migration_note: Map U-1242 to BYTE_SIZE, the U-1255 numeric-token variant to NUMERIC_VALUE, and the U-1257 alias, node, and recursion variants to the corresponding F0008 limit_kind at adoption and register callsites.

authority_locators.locator: semantic_mapping groups U-1242, U-1255, and U-1257 at load_yaml@3275 and load_yaml@3620

changes.legacy_state_or_collapse: The U-1316 strict-JSON ValueError family covers duplicate keys, non-finite values, numeric limits, nesting limits, and node-count limits before v0.4 renders a generic loader failure.
changes.proposed_v0_5_distinction: F0011 identifies only strict JSON data-model failures such as duplicate keys and non-finite values; bounded numeric, nesting, node-count, and byte limits belong to F0012.
changes.rationale: Invalid JSON data semantics and resource-budget exhaustion require different stable conditions even when the Python exception class is the same.
changes.compatibility_impact: A single legacy ValueError family becomes a typed branch between F0011 and F0012, so message-based compatibility adapters are insufficient.
changes.migration_note: At load_json@3249, map only JSON_DATA_MODEL_INVALID to F0011 and route every registered budget variant to F0012 with its required limit_kind.

authority_locators.locator: semantic_mapping group U-1316 variant JSON_DATA_MODEL_INVALID at load_json@3249

changes.legacy_state_or_collapse: JSON byte size, numeric value, nesting, and expanded node-count limits originate in U-1275, U-1316, and U-1318, while v0.4 preserves no single typed budget condition across those branches.
changes.proposed_v0_5_distinction: F0012 is the bounded JSON resource finding with a closed limit_kind of BYTE_SIZE, NUMERIC_VALUE, NESTING, or NODE_COUNT.
changes.rationale: A common resource-budget identity makes bounded parsing observable while retaining the exact exhausted dimension as structured evidence.
changes.compatibility_impact: Multiple legacy result strings consolidate into F0012 and now require a non-null, in-domain limit_kind fact.
changes.migration_note: Map U-1275 to BYTE_SIZE, U-1318 to NESTING, and the registered U-1316 budget variants to NUMERIC_VALUE, NESTING, or NODE_COUNT at load_json@3249.

authority_locators.locator: semantic_mapping groups U-1275, U-1316, and U-1318 at load_json@3249

changes.legacy_state_or_collapse: Generic schema-error producers U-SCHEMA-NONMEANINGFUL-1329 and AUX-SCHEMA-1341 are reused across documents and paths, while direct adoption-shape checks D-1656, D-3280, and D-4340 emit separate prose.
changes.proposed_v0_5_distinction: F0013 owns adoption-schema violations outside the profiles subtree; violations whose instance pointer is profiles or a descendant are separated as F0015.
changes.rationale: The general adoption schema and the declared profile-set contract have different owners and remedies even when they come from the same schema evaluator.
changes.compatibility_impact: Schema failures must be dispatched by callsite and instance pointer instead of treating all schema prose as one identity.
changes.migration_note: Map adoption schema errors outside profiles, plus D-1656, D-3280, and D-4340, to F0013; send profiles-subtree errors to F0015.

authority_locators.locator: semantic_mapping groups AUX-SCHEMA-1341 and U-SCHEMA-NONMEANINGFUL-1329 variant OTHER_ADOPTION_SCHEMA_VIOLATION, plus D-1656, D-3280, and D-4340
section 6 required local artifacts and adoption shape

changes.legacy_state_or_collapse: v0.4's generic schema failure does not provide a stable profile-declaration identity for malformed, empty, duplicated, or unknown profile selections.
changes.proposed_v0_5_distinction: F0015 is selected when the schema violation's instance pointer is profiles or one of its descendants, isolating the declared profile-set contract from other adoption schema failures.
changes.rationale: Profile classification controls downstream obligations and deserves a precise diagnostic rather than a generic adoption-schema result.
changes.compatibility_impact: A subset of legacy adoption schema failures moves from the general F0013 family to the newly distinct F0015 code.
changes.migration_note: Preserve the schema instance pointer as a typed discriminant; route profiles and descendant pointers to F0015 and every other adoption pointer to F0013.

authority_locators.locator: semantic_mapping groups AUX-SCHEMA-1341 and U-SCHEMA-NONMEANINGFUL-1329 variant PROFILE_DECLARATION_SCHEMA_VIOLATION

changes.legacy_state_or_collapse: Shared YAML parsing and schema producers, together with D-4604, yield different prose for lite-envelope, embedded-register, and loader failures and are also reused by full templates and standalone registers.
changes.proposed_v0_5_distinction: F0018 gives the adopter's lite assurance envelope and its register sections one layout-owned invalid-bundle identity, distinguished by callsite from shipped-template F0103 and standalone-register F0036.
changes.rationale: For the lite layout, the enforceable unit is the bundle envelope; callsite ownership is more stable than the generic parser or schema message that surfaced the defect.
changes.compatibility_impact: Several legacy parse, schema, and layout strings consolidate into F0018, while equivalent loader branches at other callsites produce different codes.
changes.migration_note: Attach the check_lite_file callsite before projection and map its registered loader, schema, and D-4604 envelope branches to F0018; do not reuse F0018 for shipped templates or standalone register files.

authority_locators.locator: semantic_mapping callsites load_yaml@3916 and schema_errors@3928, plus group D-4604

changes.legacy_state_or_collapse: D-3408 and D-3424 are classified as non-completion reasons for a non-real trusted directory or resource, while D-3534 is a finding for resources that differ from the pinned checkout's HEAD.
changes.proposed_v0_5_distinction: F0023 treats all safely inspected absence, type, and content mismatches in the trusted pinned resource set as one completed upstream-commit-pin finding.
changes.rationale: These states establish a repository trust-boundary violation rather than merely making an otherwise applicable check unknowable.
changes.compatibility_impact: The D-3408 and D-3424 paths change from reason semantics to a blocking finding and collapse with the existing HEAD-difference condition.
changes.migration_note: Map D-3408, D-3424, and D-3534 to F0023; callers must no longer interpret the first two as an uncompleted check once the new decision is accepted.

authority_locators.locator: semantic_mapping groups D-3408, D-3424, and D-3534

changes.legacy_state_or_collapse: The direct D-3511 Report.error for assume-unchanged or skip-worktree flags was preliminarily classified as display-only, leaving its semantic status ambiguous despite blocking behavior.
changes.proposed_v0_5_distinction: F0024 is a dedicated finding for Git index flags that can hide modifications to trusted validation resources from ordinary worktree comparison.
changes.rationale: A concealment mechanism at the trust boundary is not display decoration and must have its own stable, blocking identity.
changes.compatibility_impact: This corrects the preliminary classification and introduces a distinct code rather than folding the condition into generic trusted-resource mismatch prose.
changes.migration_note: Replace any display-only treatment of the D-3511 branch with F0024; preserve the direct producer identity and do not derive it from the rendered message.

authority_locators.locator: semantic_mapping group D-3511 and its classification_correction

changes.legacy_state_or_collapse: D-3440 treats an executing validator outside the pinned checkout as a reason, while D-3588 emits a finding when schemas and the pinned checkout resolve to different trusted trees.
changes.proposed_v0_5_distinction: F0025 unifies both cases as a completed profile-checkout-boundary mismatch.
changes.rationale: The validator, schemas, and checkout must share one trust root; a safely established mismatch is a policy violation rather than missing evaluation context.
changes.compatibility_impact: D-3440 changes from reason to blocking finding and joins D-3588 under one stable code.
changes.migration_note: Project both exact source groups D-3440 and D-3588 to F0025 after acceptance; do not preserve the former non-completion classification.

changes.legacy_state_or_collapse: The shared project-text helper emits identical cannot-read-as-UTF-8 prose for OSError and UnicodeDecodeError at multiple callsites; not-regular, oversized, and lite-file branches are separate legacy findings.
changes.proposed_v0_5_distinction: At a required-artifact callsite, inaccessible project state becomes PROJECT_TREE_UNAVAILABLE, while invalid UTF-8 or a safely established unusable required artifact becomes F0032.
changes.rationale: An inaccessible project tree prevents completion, whereas invalid content or file type is a completed artifact-conformance failure.
changes.compatibility_impact: The same legacy English text can now yield either a non-completion reason or a blocking finding, and several usable-file defects consolidate into F0032.
changes.migration_note: Dispatch D-1822 by exception type and required-artifact callsite: OSError to PROJECT_TREE_UNAVAILABLE, UnicodeDecodeError to F0032; also map D-1808, D-1815, and D-3912 artifact-usability branches to F0032.

authority_locators.locator: semantic_mapping groups D-1808, D-1815, D-1822 at read_project_text_file@3669, and D-3912

changes.legacy_state_or_collapse: For the lite system description, v0.4 emits separate missing, non-regular, oversized, empty, UTF-8, and final inline-or-file obligation prose, while the shared read helper conflates OSError with UnicodeDecodeError.
changes.proposed_v0_5_distinction: F0034 represents the safely established failure of the lite inline-or-file system-description obligation; an inaccessible project tree remains a non-completion reason.
changes.rationale: The public obligation is satisfied by either inline content or a usable file, so its stable identity should express that compound requirement while retaining the acquisition boundary.
changes.compatibility_impact: Several legacy file-state messages consolidate into F0034, but an OSError on the same helper path now produces PROJECT_TREE_UNAVAILABLE instead of a finding.
changes.migration_note: At read_project_text_file@3978, route OSError to PROJECT_TREE_UNAVAILABLE and UnicodeDecodeError plus D-1808, D-1810, D-1815, D-1825, and D-3996 to F0034 when the inline alternative is not usable.

authority_locators.locator: semantic_mapping groups D-1808, D-1810, D-1815, D-1822, D-1825 at read_project_text_file@3978, and D-3996

changes.legacy_state_or_collapse: v0.4's register path surfaces bounded YAML loading and schema evaluation through neighboring generic messages, so parse, UTF-8, resource, and actual schema states are not represented by stable typed identities.
changes.proposed_v0_5_distinction: F0036 is limited to safely loaded register values that fail their selected register schema; register YAML grammar, UTF-8, data-model, and resource-budget failures are owned by adoption.document-parse and project to F0005, F0006, F0007, or F0008.
changes.rationale: A schema check can complete only after bounded acquisition and parsing succeeds; keeping that boundary prevents malformed input from masquerading as schema nonconformance.
changes.compatibility_impact: Legacy register failures that appeared in one validation stream split into document-parse findings and the narrower F0036 schema finding, while remaining blocking when evaluation completes.
changes.migration_note: Emit F0036 only for AUX-SCHEMA-1341 or U-SCHEMA-NONMEANINGFUL-1329 at register schema callsites; route every load_yaml@3620 variant through F0005-F0008 and never recover the split from English text.

authority_locators.locator: semantic_mapping groups AUX-SCHEMA-1341 and U-SCHEMA-NONMEANINGFUL-1329 at schema_errors@2752 and schema_errors@3635

changes.legacy_state_or_collapse: Material-change workflow-root checks emit distinct path-state prose, while D-2039 uses one cannot-read-as-UTF-8 branch for both OSError and UnicodeDecodeError.
changes.proposed_v0_5_distinction: F0047 identifies a safely inspected workflow root that is malformed, outside the repository, absent, non-regular, or invalid UTF-8; inaccessible project state remains PROJECT_TREE_UNAVAILABLE.
changes.rationale: A declared root that violates the repository boundary is a completed specification-workflow finding, but a tree that cannot be inspected prevents completion.
changes.compatibility_impact: The D-2039 legacy text gains a typed reason-versus-finding split and the other root-validation messages consolidate under F0047.
changes.migration_note: At the D-2039 producer, send OSError to PROJECT_TREE_UNAVAILABLE and UnicodeDecodeError to F0047; also project D-2002, D-2020, D-2024, and D-2127 to F0047.

authority_locators.locator: semantic_mapping groups D-2002, D-2020, D-2024, D-2039, and D-2127

changes.legacy_state_or_collapse: Missing, non-regular, oversized, empty, and out-of-order root-guide states have separate v0.4 prose, while the shared D-1822 read branch collapses OSError and invalid UTF-8.
changes.proposed_v0_5_distinction: F0050 represents a safely inspected required root instruction that cannot provide the canonical visible assurance reading-order block; inaccessible project state remains a reason.
changes.rationale: The semantic obligation is the usable canonical reading-order block, not the particular helper message that caused the obligation to fail.
changes.compatibility_impact: Legacy guide-state messages consolidate into F0050, and identical D-1822 prose splits into PROJECT_TREE_UNAVAILABLE or F0050 by producer exception type.
changes.migration_note: Route OSError at read_project_text_file@1955 to PROJECT_TREE_UNAVAILABLE, UnicodeDecodeError to F0050, and map D-1808, D-1810, D-1815, D-1825, and D-1966 root-guide branches to F0050.

changes.legacy_state_or_collapse: D-4143 reports an illegal adoption stage as a reason because legacy stage requirements cannot be enforced, even though the invalid declared value was safely loaded and evaluated.
changes.proposed_v0_5_distinction: F0052 is a completed adoption.stage-conformance finding for any stage outside DRAFT, HUMAN_REVIEWED, or CONFORMANT.
changes.rationale: A safely observed invalid enum value is a policy failure, not unavailable input or an uncompleted evaluation.
changes.compatibility_impact: The D-4143 path changes from a non-completion reason to a blocking finding.
changes.migration_note: Replace the legacy requirements-cannot-be-enforced classification for D-4143 with F0052 after acceptance; retain non-completion only when the stage input itself cannot be acquired.

changes.legacy_state_or_collapse: v0.4 has separate missing and empty review-record messages, while the shared project-text read branch uses the same prose for OSError and UnicodeDecodeError.
changes.proposed_v0_5_distinction: F0063 identifies a required review record that is safely established as missing, empty, or invalid UTF-8; an inaccessible project tree remains PROJECT_TREE_UNAVAILABLE.
changes.rationale: The declared adoption stage creates a concrete record obligation, but the check must retain the distinction between a violated obligation and unavailable evaluation input.
changes.compatibility_impact: Review-record defects consolidate into F0063, while one legacy read-error message now has distinct reason and finding outcomes.
changes.migration_note: At read_project_text_file@4346, map OSError to PROJECT_TREE_UNAVAILABLE and UnicodeDecodeError to F0063; map D-4328 and D-4335, plus applicable missing or empty helper states, to F0063.

authority_locators.locator: semantic_mapping groups D-1808, D-1810, D-1815, D-1822, D-1825 at read_project_text_file@4346, plus D-4328 and D-4335

changes.legacy_state_or_collapse: The exact v0.4.0 inventory is closed with no producer for a durable human review of profile classification; declared profile syntax and stage do not establish that provenance.
changes.proposed_v0_5_distinction: F0072 is a prospective non-blocking finding when a safely loaded classification remains UNCONFIRMED; an unevaluable classification-review capability is instead non-complete and emits no F0072.
changes.rationale: Passing declared-profile gates does not prove that the project was classified correctly, and tooling must not fabricate human authority.
changes.compatibility_impact: This is a genuinely new WARN identity with a closed negative legacy inventory; future clients must tolerate it and surface human escalation without treating it as v0.4 parity.
changes.migration_note: Do not synthesize F0072 from any v0.4 message. Emit it only after the new profile-classification-review check completes as UNCONFIRMED; use a reason when the review state is UNDETERMINED.

authority_locators.locator: findings.allocated_entries[code=F0072] and its closed negative v0.4 inventory

changes.legacy_state_or_collapse: Base and head adoption, register, YAML, JSON, and path-transition failures are spread across many prose results; some loader failures are swallowed into false or compatibility fallback, and D-6605 and D-6632 are reason-classified.
changes.proposed_v0_5_distinction: F0073 is one transition.input-parse finding for safely acquired base or head policy input that is malformed, structurally invalid, or otherwise not comparable; inaccessible input remains a typed reason.
changes.rationale: Transition logic must fail closed instead of guessing absence or silently falling back when the inputs needed for comparison are invalid.
changes.compatibility_impact: A broad set of legacy strings and hidden fallback states consolidates into F0073, including selected reason-to-finding promotions, while true acquisition failure remains non-completion.
changes.migration_note: Preserve callsite and parser variant before mapping registered run_drift and load_register_document failures to F0073; suppress only the documented internal fallback duplicate and route inaccessible input to its reason code.

changes.legacy_state_or_collapse: U-4745 invalid UTF-8 and U-4778 non-canonical changed paths are upstream reason states whose rendered consumers do not expose a stable malformed changed-file-set identity.
changes.proposed_v0_5_distinction: F0095 is a completed finding for a safely acquired changed-file set that is malformed, ambiguous, invalid UTF-8, or contains a non-canonical repository-relative path.
changes.rationale: Once the input bytes are available, their invalid framing or path semantics are an evaluated transition-input violation rather than missing context.
changes.compatibility_impact: The two v0.4 reason states become a blocking finding with one stable identity.
changes.migration_note: Map U-4745 and U-4778 to F0095; reserve input-unavailable reasons for failures to acquire the changed-file input itself.

changes.legacy_state_or_collapse: U-4735, U-4763, and U-4783 expose changed-file input bytes, path length, and file-count limits as separate reason states without one typed budget result.
changes.proposed_v0_5_distinction: F0096 is the completed changed-file-set resource finding with required limit_kind INPUT_BYTES, PATH_CODE_POINTS, or CHANGED_FILE_COUNT.
changes.rationale: Bounded evaluation limits are enforceable input constraints and should expose both a stable identity and the exhausted dimension.
changes.compatibility_impact: Three non-completion reasons become a blocking finding and require an exact limit_kind fact.
changes.migration_note: Map U-4735 to INPUT_BYTES, U-4763 to PATH_CODE_POINTS, and U-4783 to CHANGED_FILE_COUNT; reject missing or unknown limit_kind values.

changes.legacy_state_or_collapse: leading_pr_directives returns impact_ambiguous or order_invalid internal state from lines 4904, 4923, and 4926, which v0.4 folds into downstream routing prose rather than an independently stable finding.
changes.proposed_v0_5_distinction: F0097 promotes a malformed or contract-violating pull-request directive block to its own transition.pr-directive-routing finding.
changes.rationale: The parser already establishes a specific invalid directive state, and downstream message wording should not determine whether that state has public identity.
changes.compatibility_impact: A previously internal or indirectly visible state becomes an independent blocking diagnostic.
changes.migration_note: Have the directive parser emit a typed invalid-state discriminant and map impact_ambiguous or order_invalid to F0097 before downstream routing prose is rendered.

changes.legacy_state_or_collapse: D-6577 classifies an oversized pull-request body as a reason, and v0.4 has no stable directive-input resource code or structured limit kind.
changes.proposed_v0_5_distinction: F0098 is a completed pull-request-directive resource finding; the mapped v0.4 producer binds limit_kind PR_BODY_BYTES, while the catalog reserves other closed directive limits.
changes.rationale: A safely measured routing input that exceeds its accepted bound is an evaluated policy failure rather than unavailable input.
changes.compatibility_impact: D-6577 changes from reason to blocking finding and gains a required structured limit_kind.
changes.migration_note: Map D-6577 to F0098 with limit_kind PR_BODY_BYTES; never infer a different reserved limit kind without a separately registered producer.

changes.legacy_state_or_collapse: Component-map shape, names, globs, and invariant-ID errors are separate v0.4 findings, while D-6894's cannot-read-as-UTF-8 text conflates OSError with UnicodeDecodeError.
changes.proposed_v0_5_distinction: F0099 consolidates safely established component-routing declaration defects; an unavailable assurance diff produces ASSURANCE_DIFF_UNAVAILABLE, while invalid UTF-8 remains a contract finding.
changes.rationale: Structural inconsistency is a completed routing-contract failure, but inability to acquire the assurance diff prevents the check from completing.

changes.legacy_state_or_collapse: Component count, name length, glob count, path length, invariant count, ID length, glob work, and mention-scan work are separate findings, while D-6885's assurance-diff byte cap is classified as a reason.
changes.proposed_v0_5_distinction: F0100 unifies all bounded component-routing exhaustion under one finding with a required limit_kind covering the nine registered work and size dimensions.

changes.proposed_v0_5_distinction: F0103 consolidates parse, data-model, bounded-resource, and schema failures at shipped-template callsites into one profile.template-conformance identity.

changes.compatibility_impact: One legacy display message splits into reason or blocking finding, and the remaining synchronization messages collapse into F0105.

changes.legacy_state_or_collapse: D-3097 uses one display-oriented CODEOWNERS read failure for OSError and UnicodeDecodeError, while D-3195 separately reports missing coverage for default adopted policy locations.
changes.proposed_v0_5_distinction: F0106 identifies invalid UTF-8 or safely established missing CODEOWNERS coverage; an OSError acquiring the template remains INPUT_FILE_UNAVAILABLE.
changes.rationale: An unreadable input and a readable but invalid or insufficient ownership policy are different completion states.
changes.compatibility_impact: The D-3097 prose splits into reason or blocking finding, and the coverage failure joins the latter under F0106.
changes.migration_note: Route D-3097 OSError to INPUT_FILE_UNAVAILABLE and UnicodeDecodeError to F0106; map D-3195 to F0106 without parsing its joined missing-path text.

authority_locators.locator: semantic_mapping source-predicate group D-3097 and direct group D-3195

changes.legacy_state_or_collapse: D-3073 uses one display-oriented archived-marker inspection message for OSError and UnicodeDecodeError, while D-3078 separately reports an exact marker-contract mismatch.
changes.proposed_v0_5_distinction: F0108 identifies invalid UTF-8 or a safely established archived-marker mismatch; failure to acquire the release input remains INPUT_FILE_UNAVAILABLE.
changes.rationale: A malformed or mismatched release artifact is a completed repository-hygiene finding, unlike an unavailable input.
changes.compatibility_impact: The D-3073 legacy display result splits into reason or blocking finding, and the mismatch message consolidates into F0108.
changes.migration_note: Dispatch D-3073 OSError to INPUT_FILE_UNAVAILABLE and UnicodeDecodeError to F0108; map D-3078 directly to F0108.

authority_locators.locator: semantic_mapping source-predicate group D-3073 and direct group D-3078

changes.legacy_state_or_collapse: U-VERSION-1756 and U-VERSION-1765 are shared by the profile release self-check and the adopter's pinned profile checkout, so low-level UTF-8 and line-framing messages do not identify which trust boundary failed.
changes.proposed_v0_5_distinction: F0113 owns invalid UTF-8, a second line, or a bare carriage return in the pinned profile checkout's VERSION resource; the release-tree callsite remains F0101.
changes.rationale: Pinned-upstream conformance and release-tree conformance are separate checks with different source references and remediation.
changes.compatibility_impact: The shared version parser family splits by callsite between F0113 and F0101.
changes.migration_note: At read_version_file@3310 in check_pinned_version, map U-VERSION-1756 and U-VERSION-1765 to F0113; at read_version_file@2774 map them to F0101.

authority_locators.locator: semantic_mapping groups U-VERSION-1756 and U-VERSION-1765 at read_version_file@3310
